reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails significantly on Content Accuracy. The user requested the specific text '别吵醒他，他工作了一整晚' (Don't wake him up, he worked all night). However, Model 1 generated '嘘，小声点，别吵醒他，他昨晚一整晚都在忙工作呢' (Shh, keep it down, don't wake him up, he was busy working all last night). It added introductory interjections and modified the sentence structure and wording extensively. Regarding the paralinguistic feature 'Volume' (音量), the model uses a soft, gentle tone. While the volume is reduced compared to normal speech, it remains fully voiced (phonated) rather than whispered. In the context of 'don't wake him', a soft voiced tone is less effective than a whisper. The expression is somewhat natural but the severe deviation from the requested text script limits the quality significantly.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 demonstrates perfect Content Accuracy, reciting the text '别吵醒他，他工作了一整晚' exactly as requested without additions or omissions. Regarding the paralinguistic feature 'Volume', Model 2 correctly interprets the instruction '小声' (small volume) in the context of avoiding waking someone up. It generates a distinct whisper (unvoiced speech), characterized by breathiness and lack of vocal cord vibration. This is the most natural and accurate way to convey the intent of the command. The fluency is high, and the prosody within the whisper is natural.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. First and foremost, Model 1 failed to follow the text constraint, hallucinating extra words and changing the sentence structure, whereas Model 2 followed the script perfectly. Secondly, regarding the target feature 'Volume', Model 2 utilized a true whisper technique, which is acoustically distinct and perfectly suits the semantic context of not waking someone up. Model 1 merely lowered the intensity of a normal voice (soft spoken) but did not whisper. Therefore, Model 2 is superior in both instruction adherence (text) and feature realization (paralinguistics).
